message:
workflows: expose WorkflowRunner::new + CxPolicyInjector::new for ft CLI

Changes two constructor visibilities from pub(crate) to pub so
cross-crate callers - specifically the ft CLI and other integration
surfaces that build a runner around a shared (engine, storage,
injector) triple - can construct workflow runners directly without
going through a feature-gated helper.

These are follow-ups to br-youhf which routed the ft CLI workflow
runners through CxPolicyInjector. That commit wired the call sites
but left the underlying constructors pub(crate), which worked only
because the ft CLI happened to live inside frankenterm-core at the
time.  Pulling the CLI out into its own crate breaks that, so this
commit promotes both constructors to pub and annotates each with a
doc comment explaining the load-bearing public contract so a future
reviewer does not silently demote them.

No behavior change; both constructors already performed the full
policy/lock/runtime setup internally.  Only the accessibility
modifier changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -292,7 +292,10 @@ pub struct WorkflowRunner {
 
 impl WorkflowRunner {
     /// Create a new workflow runner.
-    pub(crate) fn new(
+    ///
+    /// This stays public because the ft CLI and other integration surfaces
+    /// construct runners directly around a shared engine/storage/injector set.
+    pub fn new(
         engine: WorkflowEngine,
         lock_manager: Arc<PaneWorkflowLockManager>,
         storage: Arc<crate::storage::StorageHandle>,
